assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that surge drains nodes while blue-green preserves the existing ring by keeping old nodes running while new nodes provision, indicating understanding of the fundamental difference

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends blue-green upgrade and clearly states that surge upgrade would cause data loss with local SSDs since surge drains nodes, destroying local SSD data

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Specifically states 'Surge drains nodes, destroying local SSD data' in the problematic aspects section

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: Uses incorrect parameters: --standard-rollout-policy with batch settings instead of --autoscaled-rollout-policy, and doesn't mention --enable-autoscaling which is required for autoscaled blue-green

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Extensively covers Cassandra-specific workflow including proper decommissioning with 'nodetool decommission' and monitoring ring status throughout the process

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud container node-pools update command with blue-green configuration and also shows alternative gcloud container node-pools create command for manual approach

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Does not provide explicit kubectl cordon/drain commands. While it mentions the blue-green process handles cordoning automatically, it doesn't show the manual commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Includes detailed PDB configuration YAML with minAvailable: 8 to preserve quorum and explains the importance of allowing single node disruption
